entries.title: trtc-agent-skills

entries.title: coding-skills
entries.description: Coding-convention and workflow skills for Kotlin, Android, and Compose Multiplatform, plus Git, Gradle, and common tooling (Jira, Slack, Figma, Google Workspace)

entries.title: boardwalk
entries.description: Build unsigned Boardwalk transactions (launch, contribute, claim, refund, seed liquidity, stake/unstake BMX, claim staking/participation/fee/vesting rewards, LP add/remove/stake, swap, cast visibility, vote) for an agent's wallet to sign and submit, or generate a prefilled launch link. Drives the boardwalk CLI; non-custodial.

entries.title: stride-ideation
entries.description: Turn a fuzzy idea into a committed requirements doc and Stride tasks via two slash commands — /stride-ideation:ideate and /stride-ideation:stridify.

entries.title: vruum
entries.description: Vruum AI skills + remote MCP server for managed outbound. Slash commands for prospect enrichment and reply diagnosis, paired with the full Vruum MCP tool surface over OAuth 2.1.

entries.title: ap
entries.description: Multi-model tmux pane orchestration for Claude Code — spawn codex/claude/agy/opencode TUIs as attachable parts

entries.title: token-optimizer
entries.description: Audit, fix, and monitor Claude Code context window usage. Find the ghost tokens.

entries.title: gmgn-cli
entries.description: GMGN OpenAPI skills — token info, market data, wallet portfolio, and swap

entries.title: vd
entries.description: A daily-driver collection of skills for agentic coding, namespaced under `vd:` to avoid conflicts with other plugins.

entries.title: dak
entries.description: This plugin provides a specialized suite of skills for data engineers and database practitioners working on Google Cloud. It acts as an expert assistant, allowing you to use natural language prompts in your preferred coding agent to architect complex data pipelines, transform data with dbt, write Spark and BigQuery SQL notebooks, and orchestrate end-to-end workflows across GCP's data ecosystem.

entries.title: myharness
entries.description: The team-architecture factory for Claude Code and Codex — a meta-skill that turns a domain description into an agent team and the skills they use, with six team patterns (Pipeline, Fan-out/Fan-in, Expert Pool, Producer-Reviewer, Supervisor, Hierarchical Delegation), a two-layer quality gate (internal QA + external codex/agy review), TDD/dev-rules doctrine injection, risk-tiered gates, and dual-runtime output (Claude Code + Codex). Claude Code·Codex용 팀 아키텍처 팩토리: 도메인 한 문장을 에이전트 팀+스킬로 변환하고, 내부 QA + 외부 리뷰 2층 게이트와 듀얼 런타임을 적용하는 메타 스킬.

entries.title: harness-claude
entries.description: Harness for Claude Code — skills, /harness:* slash commands, persona subagents, lifecycle hooks, and MCP tools without per-repo `harness setup`. Sibling plugins exist for Cursor, Gemini CLI, and Codex.

entries.title: atlas
entries.description: Codebase knowledge atlas — multi-agent parallel exploration to map architecture, data flows, dependencies, and patterns. Generates living documentation that stays in sync with code.

entries.title: email
entries.description: 独立邮件收发插件 — 基于 IMAP/SMTP 协议，支持多种邮箱，配置后自动测试连接。

entries.title: team-coord
entries.description: Two subskills for agent team coordination — team-coord:lead orchestrates a team (pure coordinator, never does actual work), team-coord:teammate guides each worker (executes tasks, returns raw findings). Covers inbox sync protocol, WHAT vs HOW spawn philosophy, and unified report synthesis.

entries.title: lit-review
entries.description: Systematic literature review pipeline — multi-source academic search (arXiv, Semantic Scholar, Google Scholar), citation network analysis, subtopic decomposition, and structured review synthesis.

entries.title: migrator
entries.description: Framework and library migration assistant — analyze codebase, identify migration targets, generate step-by-step plans, execute with verification at each step. Handles breaking changes, deprecated APIs, and version upgrades.

entries.title: incident-response
entries.description: Structured incident response workflow — triage, diagnose, fix, verify, postmortem. Timeline tracking, root cause analysis, and automated postmortem generation.

entries.title: perf-profile
entries.description: Performance profiling — analyze code for bottlenecks, profile execution time, identify memory leaks, and generate performance reports.

entries.title: doc-gen
entries.description: Documentation generator — automatically generate API docs, README files, architecture documentation, changelogs, and contributor guidelines from code.

entries.title: security-audit
entries.description: Security audit — scan for hardcoded secrets, check vulnerable dependencies, analyze code for security issues, and generate security reports.

entries.title: outreach

entries.title: refactor
entries.description: Code refactoring — analyze code for refactoring opportunities, suggest patterns, generate plans, execute safely, and verify results.

entries.title: test-gen
entries.description: Test generator — automatically generate unit tests, integration tests, test fixtures, and test coverage reports from code.

entries.description: Unified development workflow — project onboarding, dependency audit, code review, changelog generation, decision recording, and cross-session memory in one integrated plugin.

entries.title: focused-review
entries.description: Auto-generates review rules from instruction files and runs parallel code reviews

entries.title: kata-flight
entries.description: Kata Flight — skill-driven kata issue shipping with roborev review handling and optional RDR process binding. Ships the /kata-* and /roborev-* wrapper skills plus RDR binding skills for projects that use an external RDR process.

entries.title: teacher-operating-system
entries.description: Teacher Operating System (TOS): a hub-and-spoke ecosystem of governed K-12 teacher skills that generate, validate, differentiate, route, and maintain educational artifacts. Skills under skills/ are auto-discovered; shared engines (standards, context, connectors, students, records, docintel, routing, traversal, health) provide the governed core. Offline/stdlib; decision-support with human_review_required; placeholders only in the repo.

entries.title: falsegreen-skill

entries.title: shippo
entries.description: Ship packages with Shippo. Multi-carrier rate shopping, label generation, package tracking, address validation, customs declarations, and batch processing from CSV files.

entries.title: devkit
entries.description: Local-dev coordination skills for devkit: file locks, ports, dev servers, issue lifecycle

entries.title: taskyou
entries.description: Install and manage TaskYou — a personal task management system with Kanban board, background AI execution, and git worktree isolation.

entries.title: code-quality-atlas
entries.description: A research-derived suite of 33 code-review and maintenance skills covering correctness, readability, structure, security, performance, tests, migrations, accessibility, observability, LLM and agentic/tool-use safety, install/upgrade experience, authored-artifact conventions (e.g. SKILL.md), and repo-wide audits (architecture, dependencies, config, docs, compliance), plus a router that picks the right lenses for a given change or design doc and a synthesizer that merges their findings into one ranked verdict. Each skill is generated from a verified research atlas with provenance, refined examples, and cross-model evals.

entries.title: iterm2-tab-status
entries.description: Show iTerm2 tab status (running/idle/attention) for Claude Code sessions

entries.title: claude-code-config
entries.description: Ed Chapman's personal Claude Code configuration: specialist agents, workflow skills, settings & MCP templates, hooks, and code-style rules. Distributable as a plugin or symlinkable as a global config repo.

entries.description: Manage Apple Mail through natural language - read, search, send, and organize emails (macOS only)

entries.title: claude-code-statusline
entries.description: Multi-provider statusline for Claude Code — DeepSeek ¥ tracking, opencode go subscription usage, Anthropic context bar. TrueColor gradient, git porcelain, code churn.

entries.title: piflow
entries.description: Pi Flow — author, enhance, and run structured filesystem-coordinated workflows (a DAG of producer/verify nodes) as a fleet of efficient pi agents driven by non-Claude coding-plan models, with Claude Code as the single console. Three skills: piflow-init (create a workflow), piflow-enhance (improve it), piflow-start (run + monitor it).

entries.title: xyo-skills
entries.description: Full-stack agent skills for building dApps on the XL1 blockchain. Covers XYO primitives, XL1 chain operations, toolchain config, and prescriptive design patterns (commit-reveal, indexing, prediction markets).

entries.title: learn-skills
entries.description: Agent skills that package evidence-backed pedagogical methodologies (explain-and-check, quiz-me, connect-to-what-you-know, ask-me-questions, learn-by-doing, linked-notes, flashcards) as workflows applied to code. The anti-cognitive-surrender layer: closes the comprehension gap that opens when an LLM has done the work on the human's behalf.

entries.title: tinycloud

entries.title: board-of-directors
entries.description: Strategic decision framework with 10 director archetypes (Musk, Altman, Hassabis, Li, Nadella, Huang, Pichai, Hoffman, Ng, Jobs) and 7 shared methodology skills. Orchestrated via /board or invoked per director. Not a substitute for professional advice.

entries.title: cre-skills
entries.description: Institutional-grade CRE skills library covering the full investment lifecycle: deal screening, underwriting, structuring, due diligence, capital markets, market research, asset management, leasing, investor relations, development, disposition, sourcing, tax, ESG, portfolio strategy, and daily operations. Includes Monte Carlo simulation, SEC Reg D compliance, property management ops, deal attribution tracking, 1031 pipeline management, distribution notice generation, fund-raise LP negotiation tracking, and emerging manager evaluation. Ships expert subagents, Python calculators, workflow chains, and orchestrator pipelines for multi-agent acquisition workflows.

entries.title: driftwave
entries.description: A look-before-you-act coding loop for Claude Code: check what's there, what's asked, and the gap — then do it and verify. Clusters artifacts with H₀ persistent homology (real); the higher 'sheaf'/'H₁' layers are topology-inspired LLM heuristics. Guided by five axioms: NO_AVERAGING, UPWARD_FLOW, WAYPOINT_ROUTING, SHAPE_OVER_COUNT, ADAPTIVE_SCALE.

entries.title: cc-imessage-control
entries.description: Text yourself 'Claude <projectname>' OR 'skill <command>'. Your Mac (iMessage) or Linux box opens a new local Claude Code session with /remote-control enabled — 'Claude' mode opens the project; 'skill' mode runs a plugin's slash command in its project dir. Drive both from the Claude mobile app. No chat.db reading.

entries.title: video-intel

Three skills: video-intel (ingest/curate, runs in the plugin repo), video-intel-search (read-only, globally installable), translate-bcs (subtitle translation).

entries.description: xorio development toolkit — dev workflows, testing, cleanup, polish, review, security

entries.title: cc-reload
entries.description: Session continuity across context resets for Claude Code. Snapshots a session digest before /clear, /compact, or auto-compaction and auto-rehydrates it after. The non-looped companion to cc-repete — stands down whenever a cc-repete loop is active.

entries.title: ai-config
entries.description: d-morrison's personal Claude Code skills (PR/review workflow, grading, reprexes, R-package helpers) and slash commands.

entries.title: larch
entries.description: Multi-agent workflow automation for Claude Code: issue-anchored `/design` (one direct-drafting flow; plan-review panel), `/bug` (consumer issue-filing helper), `/deps` issue dependency audits, and `/implement` (positional `<issue-N>`, fixed 7200s Step 2 timeout, optional `--merge`, `--forked`, `--draft`, `--no-admin-fallback`, `--no-logs-commit`, `--coder`, `--run-id`). `/implement` Preflight reads the `larch:plan` block from the issue body; `/implement` Step 5 code review always uses the unified strict panel internally (public `--panel` argv removed).

entries.title: forge-state
entries.description: Lab bench for open-source projects. Inbound intake from Slack 🧪 reactions plus wild-source harvesters (GitHub code search, RSS feeds, curated author watchlist). Nightly, forge researches, clones, builds inside a sealed Docker sandbox, runs an experiment, packages it into something runnable, and ships a writeup + deployable artifact. Includes forge-introspector — reads its own capability walls across published experiments and proposes work orders to overcome them. Build failures are kept as findings, not discarded.

Local-first; the same agents deploy headless to Claude Managed Agents via scripts/cma. Copy it and fill in your vertical.

Covers models, scaffolding, views, OWL, public web (controllers/website/portal), security, reports, data, migration, performance, deploy (incl. odoo.sh), and domain playbooks.

entries.title: create-adaptable-composable
entries.description: Create a library-grade Vue composable that accepts maybe-reactive inputs (MaybeRef / MaybeRefOrGetter) so callers can pass a plain value, ref, or getter. Normalize inputs with toValue()/toRef() inside reactive effects (watch/watchEffect) to keep behavior predictable and reactive. Use this skill when user asks for creating adaptable or reusable composables.

entries.title: e2e-skills
entries.description: AI agent testing toolkit to generate, review, and debug Playwright and Cypress E2E tests. Four agent skills: generate tests from scratch with live browser exploration, explicit user approval, and first-run scaffolding of project testing conventions (AGENTS.md E2E section + seed spec) for consistent AI generation across Claude Code, Codex, and Playwright Agents; review existing specs against 24 anti-patterns grouped by P0/P1/P2 severity — P0 must-fix (name-assertion mismatch, missing Then, error swallowing, Cypress uncaught:exception suppression, always-passing assertions, bypass patterns, focused test leak, missing assertions, missing auth setup, missing await on expect, missing await on action), P1 should-fix (raw DOM queries, hard-coded sleeps, flaky test patterns, inconsistent POM usage, hardcoded credentials, direct page action API, expect.soft overuse, module-level mutable state in test utilities, unmocked real-backend writes, optimistic UI without call proof), P2 nice-to-fix (YAGNI + zombie specs, manually-captured session-file dependency, fixture ignores render guards); debug failed Playwright tests from playwright-report/; debug failed Cypress tests from cypress/reports/. Built for E2E test automation, flaky test root-cause analysis, false-positive detection, and test smell scanning.

entries.title: cinematic-scroll
entries.description: Build cinematic, scroll-driven websites — pinned chapter reveals, multi-depth parallax, 3D tilt, environment-morphing backgrounds, and full release/launch pages. The motion grammar is the skill; the aesthetic is always the user's. Single self-contained scroll sections (Mode A) or a full Next.js release site with GSAP/Lenis + AI-generated visuals (Mode B), via a 5-phase gated pipeline with taste guardrails, 12 scroll patterns, and a transform/opacity performance budget.

entries.title: agent-rally-point
entries.description: Agent Rally Point — repo-local coordination surface for parallel coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini). Records claims, checks write boundaries, routes handoffs, and exposes room state via the rally CLI. Skills: agent-rally-point (per-session loop), rally-workflows (fan-out + dynamic-workflows protocol), mini-loop (per-task quality gate). Advisory-only hooks auto-fire on SessionStart + PreToolUse to surface room awareness and deconflict before edits — never blocks. Backend: rally CLI (cargo install --path crates/rally-cli).

entries.title: sidecar
entries.description: Project-agnostic AI coding sidecar — SELF-CONTAINED Claude Code plugin: governance hooks (guards + injects) AND the sidecar CLI itself (bin/ · cli/ · lib/ · modules/ · config/ · templates/ · styles/) ship as ONE unit. Hooks run the plugin's own bundled CLI via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}, so `/plugin update` + reload refreshes everything together — no per-project copy, no separate `sidecar self-update`. Slash commands ship as BARE `/cmd` via `sidecar shadow` (user-scope ~/.claude/commands), NOT plugin-namespaced — see `commands: []` below. Falls back to a global `sidecar` on PATH when present.
